investorAngle: Cooling prices with rising inventory create negotiating leverage for buyers and investors hunting for selective value-add deals.
sellerAngle: Homes that are priced sharply and marketed cleanly can still move, but aspirational pricing is getting punished.
buyerAngle: Buyers have more room to negotiate on credits, repairs, and timing than they did a year ago.

investorAngle: Demand is still supported by migration and lifestyle appeal, but higher carrying costs mean investors need tighter underwriting.
sellerAngle: Sellers still benefit from long-term demand, but buyers are comparing options carefully and reacting fast to overpriced inventory.
buyerAngle: Selection has improved, giving buyers more choices even in premium neighborhoods.

investorAngle: A more balanced market favors disciplined operators who buy for cash flow rather than pure appreciation.
sellerAngle: Well-presented homes are still getting attention, but speed depends heavily on pricing and product quality.
buyerAngle: Buyers have enough inventory to be patient without losing the best properties if they act decisively.
